title: ATMTA Historic Expense Reimbursement
brief: This proposal seeks reimbursement of $469,513.53 USD from the Star Atlas DAO Treasury for verified historic expenditures paid by ATMTA on behalf of the Star Atlas DAO covering 2021 tax obligations and payments to two professional services providers.
description:
# PIP-33: ATMTA Historic Expense Reimbursement

**Category:** Funding
**Author:** Michael Wagner, on behalf of ATMTA, Inc.
**Type:** DAO Treasury
**Date:** June 2026

---

## 2. Summary

This proposal seeks reimbursement of $469,513.53 USD from the Star Atlas DAO Treasury for verified historic expenditures paid by ATMTA on behalf of the Star Atlas DAO covering 2021 tax obligations and payments to two professional services providers.

Reimbursement structured as 75% USDC ($352,135.15) and 25% ATLAS (~$117,378.38 equivalent), split into two equal semi-annual tranches of $234,756.76 each.

Given this exceeds the standard Ecosystem Fund limit, the Star Atlas Foundation is coordinating to bring this as an extraordinary funding measure directly to community vote. Outcome remains entirely in the hands of POLIS holders.

---

## 3. Motivation

During the early phases of the DAO's establishment, before governance infrastructure and treasury processes were fully operational, ATMTA absorbed several significant costs on the DAO's behalf:

|Category|Amount|
|-|-|
|I. 2021 Taxes|$318,092|
|II. Walkers|$142,780|
|III. Leeward|$8,641|
|**Total**|**$469,514**|

These were obligation-level payments required for the DAO's continued lawful and operational existence, not discretionary development costs. ATMTA made them in good faith without the expectation the DAO would eventually reimburse them. However, given continued market under-performance, this capital is now material in business continuity, particularly in light of a significantly reduced cost structure of our team. We believe this reimbursement extends runway by 6-12 months, depending on other internal expense decisions.

Aligns with the DAO's founding principle of **Sustainable Governance**: the DAO should fairly compensate those who supported it.

---

## 4. Specification

Two equal semi-annual tranches:

**Tranche 1 — $234,756.76**

* USDC: $176,067.57
* ATLAS: equivalent to $58,689.19 USD at 7-day VWAP on day prior to execution

**Tranche 2 — $234,756.76** *(180 days after Tranche 1)*

* Same structure

ATLAS pricing: 7-day VWAP of ATLAS/USDC from a designated oracle (e.g., Birdeye / Jupiter TWAP) on the business day preceding each payment, which protects both DAO and ATMTA from short-term manipulation.

* Special Note on Tranche 2: We will assess the value of assets in the DAO Treasury at the time the second payment is due, and only deduct a maximum value up to an amount that retains sufficient capital to cover an additional year of operating costs for the Foundation/DAO.

---

## 5. Stakeholder Impact

|Stakeholder|Benefit|Risk|
|-|-|-|
|POLIS Holders|Settles legitimate obligation, strengthens DAO integrity|Treasury expenditure of $469,513|
|ATMTA|Recoups capital advanced on DAO's behalf|—|
|Foundation|Demonstrates capacity to facilitate extraordinary measures|May face scrutiny given ATMTA's role as sponsor|
|Community|Transparency into early cost management|Perception risk surrounding Material Conflict of Interest in ATMTA writing the proposal and being the sole beneficiary, as well as negative impact to future proposals requesting funding from the Ecosystem Fund|

---

## 6. Implementation Details

Source: DAO Treasury (`CuwnarNh7FEqZMmffFjRyWj54RecyS7zwFg1CxfzNudi`)

**Proposal Draft Period:**

1. **T+0 days:** ATMTA publishes expense documentation (tax filings, invoices, ledger summary) to governance forum at the time proposal draft is submitted (complete)

**Upon Proposal Passing:**

* **T+7 days:** ATMTA confirms and publishes recipient treasury address
* **T+14 days (Tranche 1):** Foundation calculates ATLAS VWAP → executes USDC + ATLAS transfers → publishes on-chain confirmation
* **T+194 days (Tranche 2):** Same process repeated
* **T+194 days:** Foundation posts final transaction link confirmation closing the proposal

No on-chain program changes required — standard SPL token transfers from DAO Treasury.

---

## 7. Timeline

|Milestone|Action|
|-|-|
|Proposal Draft Period||
|T+0|Proposal approved (complete)|
|T+0|Documentation published (complete)|
|Upon Proposal Passing||
|T+7|Recipient address confirmed|
|T+14|**Tranche 1** released ($176,067.57 USDC + $58,689.19 ATLAS equiv.)|
|T+14|Tranche 1 on-chain confirmation posted|
|T+194|**Tranche 2** released|
|T+194|Final transaction link confirmation|

---

## 8. Cost & Resource Allocation

|Item|USD|USDC (75%)|ATLAS Equiv. (25%)|
|-|-|-|-|
|2021 Taxes|$318,092.47|$238,569.35|$79,523.12|
|Walkers|$142,779.80|$107,084.85|$35,694.95|
|Leeward|$8,641.26|$6,480.94|$2,160.32|
|**Total**|**$469,513.53**|**$352,135.15**|**$117,378.38**|

One-time only. No ongoing costs.

*Note on limits: This exceeds the standard Ecosystem Fund cap. The two-tranche structure ensures measured, milestone-based disbursement rather than a lump sum.

---

## 9. Additional Details
### Conflict of Interest

**Material Conflict of Interest — full disclosure required.**

ATMTA is both the proposal author (through CEO Michael Wagner) and the sole beneficiary. This is a direct conflict of interest.

**Mitigations:**

* Full public disclosure here
* Supporting documentation published before any funds released
* Star Atlas Council independently verifies documentation before authorizing execution
* POLIS holders retain full authority to reject — Foundation coordination ≠ guaranteed approval
* Recipient address publicly confirmed before transfers

Earlier versions: N/A — first submission.

---
